summary:
All six open UI findings are verified present in the current code, and all four code-quality fixes are confirmed applied — exactly matching the existing report's claims. The report at the declared output path already accurately compiles the three critics' verdicts (requirements: **pass**; guide-alignment: **needs_work**, six minor items; code-quality: **needs_work** on record, all four findings verified resolved) and correctly frames the recommendation to merge with minor follow-ups.

Since the report's every factual claim checks out against the current tree, the only remaining question is whether it fully reflects this iteration's verdicts. It does — the verdicts are unchanged from what the report already documents. No edits are warranted; rewriting identical, verified content would add nothing.

Summary: I compiled the review by reading the step's `input.json` (which pointed to the review phase, with the six guide-alignment findings echoed as feedback) and the three critics' verdicts — `requirements-conformance-critic` (pass, 50/50 requirements), `guide-alignment-critic` (needs_work, six minor findings F1–F6), and `code-quality-critic` (needs_work, four findings, no correctness/security defects). I then verified the existing `review_report.md` against the current source tree: confirmed all four code-quality fixes are present (`broadcast_card.rb` `dashboard:` flag, DB-level `order/limit` in `recent_activity.rb`, the union stuck+unstuck broadcast in `sweep.rb`, and the single guarded `Dashboard::Broadcast` in `manager_tick.rb`), and confirmed all six open UI/style-guide findings still exist (red-500/green-500 palette drift and off-scale spacing in `_pipeline_row.html.erb`, the inline attention pill, the run-selection logic in `define_helper.rb`, and the poll-frame fleet panel). The report accurately maps every verdict — what was asked, what was built, evidence of conformance, resolved code-quality findings, and the six minor open items — to the current state, so it
